title: mentha × verticillata f. fallax
intro: Mentha × verticillata f. fallax, commonly known as the peacock mint or pineapple mint, is a cultivar of the mint plant belonging to the Lamiaceae family. It is a hardy perennial herb known for its unique, fragrant leaves and its ability to repel pests and attract pollinators. The medicinal properties of Mentha × verticillata f. fallax include its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to alleviate digestive issues, colds, and flu. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to soothe digestive issues, relieve headaches and migraines, and boost the immune system, and it can be consumed as a tea, added to salads, or used in herbal preparations such as tinctures and essential oils.
